senses.definition: A misspelling or variant plural referring to Pyracantha, a genus of evergreen shrubs; not a standard separate sense in English dictionaries.
examples: The gardener planted several pyracanthas along the fence.

senses.definition: A plural form referring to Pyracantha shrubs, commonly known as firethorns; used in gardening and botany.
examples: Pyracanths produce bright berries in autumn.

senses.definition: A family name in plant taxonomy, referring to the subfamily or family Pyraceae commonly associated with pepper family groupings in older classifications; used in botany.
examples: Pyraceae includes several genera like Piper and Peperomia.

senses.definition: A chemical compound with the given molecular formula, known in chemistry literature as pyracene.
examples: Pyracene was synthesized and analyzed in the study.

senses.definition: A historical or specialized printing technique; often encountered in art or publishing contexts.
examples: The old magazine was produced by pyragravure plates.

senses.definition: A member of the Pyralidae family, a group of moths; also used as a morphological descriptor in entomology.
examples: The pyral moths were attracted to the light trap.

senses.definition: An order of moths in entomology (historical classification); encountered in scientific texts.
examples: Pyrales species were studied in the field guide.

senses.definition: A moth of the family Pyralidae; often used in entomology and agriculture contexts.
examples: A pyralid larva was found on stored grain.

senses.definition: A family of moths known as the snout moths or grass miners; includes many species.
examples: Pyralidae is a large family of moths with diverse habits.

senses.definition: Relating to the Pyralidae family of moths; used in descriptive entomology.
examples: A pyralid antechamber moth was observed.

senses.definition: A member of the Pyralidoidea superfamily within Lepidoptera; used in taxonomy.
examples: The specimen belonged to a pyralidid group.

senses.definition: A higher rank grouping (superfamily Pyralidoidea or family-level) within snout moths; used in entomology.
examples: Pyralididae includes several genera of moths.

senses.definition: Moths belonging to the Pyralididae family; plural form used in scientific writing.
examples: Pyralidids are common in stored product environments.

senses.definition: Resembling or related to Pyralidae moths; used in scientific description.
examples: Pyralidiform wings suggest a close relation to snout moths.

senses.definition: A superfamily in moths that includes the family Pyralidae; used in taxonomy.
examples: Pyralidoidea comprises several families of moths.

senses.definition: Moths of the family Pyralidae; the plural form used in entomology.
examples: Pyralids infest stored grains.

senses.definition: A taxonomic genus within Pyralidae or related groups; used in scientific naming.
examples: Pyralis is a genus of moths described in a taxonomic key.

senses.definition: Third-person singular simple present form of pyralize, a rare or specialized term in entomology or taxonomy; may not be widely used.
examples: The author pyralises the specimen in the notes.

senses.definition: A moth of the superfamily Pyraloidea; or an adjective describing moths of that group.
examples: Pyraloids are common in certain ecosystems.

senses.definition: A taxonomic genus within Lepidoptera; used in scientific descriptions of certain moths.
examples: Pyrameis species were cataloged in the monograph.

senses.definition: A solid shape with a polygon base and triangular sides that meet at a point; often used as a temple or monument shape.
examples: The pyramid in Egypt is famous.

senses.definition: A structure with layers that become smaller toward the top; used metaphorically for a hierarchy.
examples: The corporate pyramid has many levels.

senses.definition: To form or arrange into a pyramid shape.
examples: They pyramided the blocks into a tall tower.

senses.definition: A term less common in modern usage; may refer to a decorative or architectural pyramid-related form or project name.
examples: The architect studied the pyramidaire motif.

senses.definition: Relating to or shaped like a pyramid.
examples: The mountain has a pyramidal peak.

senses.definition: A Latin-rooted or scientific form meaning relating to a pyramid; used in biological Latin names or descriptions.
examples: Pyramidale features in the botanical description.

senses.definition: A species epithet or taxonomic name in biology; used in Latin binomials (e.g., species name pyramidalis).
examples: Quercus pyramidalis is a hypothetical name used for illustration.

senses.definition: A female or male form (rarely used) of the Latin-derived epithet pyramidalis in taxonomy.
examples: The specimen was labeled pyramidalis in the catalog.
